question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the iteration order of HashMultimap.keySet() (which is non-deterministic) to cause non-deterministic consensus outcomes where different runs of validate() on the same input accept different hash groups as having reached consensus, leading to inconsistent smart contract state on the mirror node?
